segments.text: Sometime after this, Joseph was told,
Your father is sick.
segments.speaker: Sometime after this, Joseph was

segments.text: So Joseph went to see him, taking with him his two sons Manasseh and Ephraim.
When Jacob was told,
Your son Joseph has come to you,
segments.speaker: Jacob was

segments.text: he gathered his strength and sat up in bed.
Jacob said to Joseph,
God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in Canaan, and he blessed me there.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: He told me, ‘Listen! I will make you prosperous and make your descendants so numerous that you will become the ancestor of many nations, and I will give this land to your descendants to own forever.’
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: I am counting as mine your two sons Ephraim and Manasseh who were born here in Egypt before I arrived, just as Reuben and Simeon are mine.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Any other children you have after them will be yours, and will share their inheritance within the land of their older brothers.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: I'm doing this because[^178] tragically for me when I was returning from Paddan-aram, Rachel died in Canaan some distance from Ephrath. I buried her there on the way to Ephrath
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: (also known as Bethlehem).
Israel saw Joseph's sons and said,
So these are your sons?
segments.speaker: Israel saw Joseph's sons and

segments.text: Yes, these are the sons God gave me here,
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Joseph told his father.
Bring them over here so I can bless them,
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: he said.
Israel's eyesight was failing because of his age and he couldn't see well, so Joseph brought them close to his father, and he kissed and hugged them.
Israel said to Joseph,
I never thought I'd see your face again, and now God has even let me see your children!
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: Joseph took his sons from between Israel's knees, and bowed low with his face to the ground.
Then Joseph placed Ephraim on his right so he would be on Israel's left, and Manasseh on his left so he would be on Israel's right, and then brought them over to Israel.
But when Israel reached out his hands, he crossed them over and placed his right hand on Ephraim the younger son, and placed his left on Manasseh, the firstborn.
He blessed Joseph, saying,
May the God my grandfather Abraham and my father worshiped—the God who has taken care of me like a shepherd throughout my life until now,
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: the Angel who has saved me from all kinds of trouble—may he bless these boys. May my name and the names of my grandfather Abraham and father Isaac continue through them, and may they have many descendants that spread throughout the earth.
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: Joseph was unhappy when he saw his father had put his right hand on Ephraim, so he took his father's hand to try and move it from Ephraim's head to Manasseh's head.
Not like that, father, this is the firstborn; put your right hand on his head,
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Joseph told him.
But his father refused, saying,
I know what I'm doing. Manasseh will also become an important people, but his younger brother will be greater than him, and his descendants will become a large nation.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: So Israel blessed them that day and said:
In the future the people of Israel will use your names to give a blessing, saying, ‘May God bless you like he did Ephraim and Manasseh.’
segments.speaker: Israel blessed them that day and

segments.text: In saying this he placed Ephraim before Manasseh.
Then Israel said to Joseph,
I'm going to die soon, but God will be with you and bring you back to the land of your fathers.
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: I'm also giving you something in addition to what I'm giving your brothers—the piece of land on the mountain slope of Shechem[^179] that I took from the Amorites with my sword and bow.
segments.speaker: Israel
